tagline: Fullmetal Alchemist — Every creation demands equal destruction in a rigged thermodynamic arms race.
visualizationReason: FMAB is structurally defined by parasitic control networks — Father routes energy through a hidden layer beneath Amestris, homunculi enforce institutional dependency, and alchemists unknowingly operate on a hijacked grid. The node-graph renderer exposes who controls whom, which nodes bypass the firewall, and how the counter-network assembles to dismantle the parasite from within.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: One is All, and All is One.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [THERMODYNAMIC_BREACH]: EQUIVALENT EXCHANGE VIOLATION DETECTED — GATE TOLL PENDING

aiInsights.casual: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood runs on one absolute rule: to get something, you must give something of equal value. Two brothers tried to bring back their dead mother using alchemy and paid with their bodies. Now they're searching for the Philosopher's Stone to undo the damage — but the Stone is made of human souls, and the entire country of Amestris has been secretly engineered as a giant transmutation circle by an ancient entity called Father. The node-graph shows how Father's network controls the military, the homunculi, and even the energy source that alchemists think they're drawing from — while a scattered resistance of foreign alchemists, rogue soldiers, and the brothers themselves assembles to break the system from within.
aiInsights.deep: FMAB operates as a closed thermodynamic system with a parasitic overlay. Father intercepts the energy layer between alchemists and the planet's tectonic crust, substituting his own Philosopher's Stone as the true power source — making every Amestrian transmutation a dependency call to his hidden infrastructure. The node-graph exposes this architectural parasitism: the State Military functions as an unwitting construction crew for a 400-year transmutation circle, the homunculi enforce compartmentalized control at each institutional node, and the alchemists operate on a hijacked grid they believe is natural. The counter-network assembles through foreign protocol injection (Alkahestry bypasses Father's firewall entirely), decentralized failsafe deployment (Hohenheim's counter-circle), and hybrid nodes (Scar's blended destruction/routing, Ling-Greed's human-homunculus symbiosis). The system's core tension is between centralized extraction and distributed resilience — Father's authoritarian consumption of souls versus Hohenheim's cooperative dialogue with his internal network.

powerSystem.name: Amestrian Alchemy
powerSystem.subtitle: State-Sanctioned Transmutation Engine
powerSystem.loreDesc: The dominant science of Amestris — practitioners use geometric Transmutation Circles to deconstruct and reconstruct matter by channeling energy from the Earth's tectonic plates. Requires precise understanding of atomic composition. Publicly regulated through the State Alchemist program, which licenses practitioners as military assets.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A channeled energy protocol that routes tectonic force through geometric matrices to execute matter transformations. Unknown to its users, the actual energy source is Father's subterranean Philosopher's Stone network — making every transmutation a dependency call to a parasitic infrastructure layer that can be revoked at will.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Edward performing circleless transmutation for the first time — clapping his hands to complete the circuit through his own body, bypassing the standard geometric interface entirely.
powerSystem.loreSubtitle: The Science of Deconstruction and Reconstruction

powerSystem.name: Alkahestry
powerSystem.subtitle: Eastern Chi-Based Transmutation
powerSystem.loreDesc: The Xingese healing art that channels the Dragon's Pulse — natural life energy flowing through the Earth's topography. Uses a universal Purification Circle based on the Wu Xing pentagram. Capable of long-range transmutation through alkahestric markers and specialized in medical restoration rather than destruction.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An independent energy protocol that connects to the planet's organic chi network, completely bypassing Father's artificial energy layer. Its universal geometry and long-range routing capability make it structurally immune to the denial-of-service attack that neutralizes Amestrian alchemy.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: May Chang performing long-range healing transmutation using kunai as routing markers — demonstrating that alkahestry operates on an entirely separate grid from Amestrian alchemy.

powerSystem.name: Philosopher's Stone
powerSystem.subtitle: Condensed Soul Battery
powerSystem.loreDesc: A hyper-dense alchemical amplifier composed of trapped human souls. Appears to bypass Equivalent Exchange by generating massive transmutations without visible input — but the cost is internalized as the violent consumption of the souls within. Finite, degradable, and the root cause of every atrocity in the series.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A high-capacity energy reservoir that internalizes transmutation costs by burning condensed soul-units. Eliminates the need for transmutation circles and vastly amplifies output, but introduces a hard depletion ceiling — when the soul count reaches zero, the Stone dissolves and the user loses all augmentation instantly.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Father using the combined souls of Xerxes to power himself for four centuries — demonstrating both the Stone's terrifying capacity and its ultimate fragility when the internal souls rebel.

powerSystem.name: The Gate of Truth
powerSystem.subtitle: Universal Cryptographic Ledger
powerSystem.loreDesc: A metaphysical boundary that stores the totality of alchemical knowledge. When an alchemist commits a taboo transmutation, they are dragged before Truth — a featureless entity that enforces Equivalent Exchange at the cosmic level, extracting an ironic physical toll perfectly mirroring the alchemist's deepest desire.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The universe's root-level enforcement mechanism — a cryptographic ledger that audits taboo operations, forcibly transfers universal knowledge to the violator, and extracts a precisely calibrated physical toll as payment. Survivors retain an open connection to the universal code, enabling circleless transmutation.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Edward sacrificing his own Gate of Truth to retrieve Alphonse's body — trading his ability to perform alchemy forever in exchange for his brother, the only transaction Truth acknowledges with a smile.
powerSystem.loreSubtitle: The White Void Beyond the Gate

rankings.topTierLore: The featureless entity at the Gate that enforces Equivalent Exchange at the cosmic level. Truth is not a god with intent — it is the living embodiment of the universe's accounting system, extracting perfectly ironic tolls from anyone who attempts to bypass the thermodynamic ledger.
rankings.topTierSystem: The root-level system administrator with absolute audit authority. Truth cannot be bargained with, corrupted, or overridden — it represents the hard ceiling of the system, punishing every unauthorized access attempt with a precisely calculated resource deduction.

tiers.name: Father (Parasitic Overlord)
tiers.loreDesc: The Dwarf in the Flask who consumed half of Xerxes and built Amestris as a 400-year transmutation circuit. Commands the homunculi, controls the energy grid, and seeks to absorb God itself — but his centralized architecture makes him vulnerable to distributed counter-measures.
tiers.systemDesc: A parasitic root-level process that hijacked the planetary energy grid and constructed an entire nation as infrastructure for a single extraction event. Maximum raw power but catastrophic single-point-of-failure architecture.

tiers.name: Van Hohenheim (Distributed Counter-Architect)
tiers.loreDesc: Bound to the same Xerxesian souls as Father but operating through cooperative dialogue rather than authoritarian suppression. Spent centuries deploying counter-measures throughout Amestris to neutralize Father's ritual at the moment of activation.
tiers.systemDesc: A decentralized counter-network with equivalent energy capacity to the parasitic node but superior resilience. Cooperative soul management enables precision failsafe deployment that exploits the adversary's rigid execution timing.

tiers.name: State Alchemists (Licensed Weapons)
tiers.loreDesc: Military-commissioned practitioners who trade research freedom and state funding for deployment as human weapons. The program serves a dual purpose: militarizing alchemy and filtering for candidates who might attempt Human Transmutation — creating the Human Sacrifices Father requires.
tiers.systemDesc: Institutionally captured nodes whose energy access depends entirely on the parasitic layer. High individual output but total systemic vulnerability — Father's denial-of-service instantly renders them inert.

tiers.name: Homunculi (Compartmentalized Enforcers)
tiers.loreDesc: Seven artificial beings created from Father's purged sins, each powered by a Philosopher's Stone. They enforce the conspiracy through infiltration, assassination, construction, and surveillance — but their regeneration is finite and their loyalty is hardcoded, not chosen.
tiers.systemDesc: Specialized sub-routines with dedicated functions: surveillance (Pride), labor (Sloth), destabilization (Envy), administration (Wrath). High individual resilience but fundamentally dependent on the parent process — when Father falls, the entire sub-routine network collapses.

characters.name: Edward Elric
characters.title: The System Hacker
characters.loreBio: A prodigy who committed the taboo of Human Transmutation at age eleven, losing his leg and then his arm to bind his brother's soul to armor. Certified as the youngest State Alchemist in history, Edward uses the military's resources to search for the Philosopher's Stone while systematically uncovering the conspiracy that built Amestris as a slaughterhouse.
characters.systemBio: A rare surviving taboo-executor who retains an open Gate connection, enabling circleless transmutation through somatic circuit-completion. Operates as a subversive node within the institutional framework — leveraging State Alchemist privileges to map and dismantle the parasitic infrastructure from inside the host system.
characters.signatureMoment: Sacrificing his own Gate of Truth to retrieve Alphonse's body — trading his ability to perform alchemy forever, the only toll Truth accepts with approval.
characters.description: Edward Elric is the youngest State Alchemist in history, a prodigy who sacrificed his arm and leg in a forbidden transmutation to save his brother Alphonse, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
relationships: Fullmetal Alchemist: The Conqueror of Shamballa
Fullmetal Alchemist: The Sacred Star of Milos

characters.name: Alphonse Elric
characters.title: The Bound Anomaly
characters.loreBio: Edward's younger brother whose entire physical body was consumed as the Gate's toll. His soul, bound to a suit of armor by Edward's desperate sacrifice, exists as a walking contradiction — a consciousness without biological needs, unable to eat, sleep, or feel. His armored form conceals a gentle intellect capable of sophisticated alchemy.
characters.systemBio: A disembodied consciousness anchored to inorganic matter through blood-seal binding — an anomalous persistence state that violates standard biological constraints. Retains Gate access and circleless capability, functioning as a secondary taboo-survivor node with unique durability but catastrophic vulnerability at the seal point.
characters.signatureMoment: Sacrificing his own soul to restore Edward's arm during the final battle — trusting that his brother will find a way to bring him back, mirroring their original transmutation in reverse.
characters.description: Alphonse Elric is Edward's younger brother whose soul is bound to a suit of armor after a catastrophic human transmutation went wrong, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
relationships: Fullmetal Alchemist: The Conqueror of Shamballa
Fullmetal Alchemist: The Sacred Star of Milos

characters.name: Father
characters.title: The Parasitic Root Process
characters.loreBio: Originally the Dwarf in the Flask — a sentient homunculus born in ancient Xerxes who manipulated a king into activating a nationwide transmutation circle, consuming a million souls. He built Amestris over four centuries as infrastructure for a second, larger ritual: absorbing God itself. He controls the nation's energy grid, commands seven homunculi, and has engineered every major war as a node in his transmutation array.
characters.systemBio: The root-level malicious process that architected an entire nation as a single-purpose extraction circuit. Operates as a parasitic energy layer between alchemists and the planetary crust, substituting his own Philosopher's Stone as their power source. His centralized, authoritarian architecture maximizes raw throughput but creates catastrophic fragility — when the internal soul network rebels, the entire system collapses inward.
characters.signatureMoment: Activating the Nationwide Transmutation Circle on the Promised Day — absorbing fifty million Amestrian souls and pulling God into his body, achieving momentary omnipotence before the counter-network tears it away.
characters.description: Father is a homunculus who plot to become a god by draining the power of an entire nation through a nationwide transmutation circle, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.

characters.name: Roy Mustang
characters.title: The Institutional Reformer
characters.loreBio: A war veteran who participated in the Ishvalan genocide as a human weapon, then vowed to reform the system from within by becoming Führer. His flame alchemy — controlling oxygen density to generate precision firestorms — makes him one of the most destructive alchemists alive. His guilt drives him to dismantle the military apparatus that weaponized him.
characters.systemBio: A high-output combat node specializing in atmospheric oxygen manipulation for thermal destruction. Operates as an institutional reform vector — leveraging his position within the corrupt hierarchy to build a parallel loyalty network aimed at regime replacement. His guilt-driven optimization function makes him a predictable but highly motivated counter-system actor.
characters.signatureMoment: Incinerating Lust through repeated, unbroken cycles of flame — burning through her Philosopher's Stone regeneration cycle by cycle until her soul reservoir is completely depleted.
characters.description: Roy Mustang is the Flame Alchemist, an ambitious State Alchemist who commands fire with precise precision and dreams of becoming Führer to reform Amestris, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
relationships: Fullmetal Alchemist: The Conqueror of Shamballa
Fullmetal Alchemist: The Sacred Star of Milos

characters.name: King Bradley
characters.title: The Administrative Interface
characters.loreBio: A human-homunculus hybrid engineered by Father to serve as the political interface between the shadow conspiracy and the Amestrian state. His human body fought and destroyed all but one wrathful soul, leaving him without regeneration but with the Ultimate Eye — hyper-predictive vision that makes him virtually untouchable in combat. He is the banality of evil made flesh.
characters.systemBio: A unique hybrid node combining homunculus-grade combat optimization with human biological constraints. Lacks the regenerative loop of his siblings due to single-soul architecture, compensating with hyper-predictive sensory processing. Functions as the administrative bridge between Father's shadow network and the institutional military apparatus — the human face of systemic control.
characters.signatureMoment: Single-handedly storming the front gate of Central Command during the coup — cutting through dozens of soldiers while aging and wounded, demonstrating that his mortal body fights with conviction rather than regeneration.
characters.description: King Bradley is the Führer President of Amestris, secretly a homunculus with superhuman reflexes and blades concealed in his eyes, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.

characters.name: Scar
characters.title: The Hybrid Insurgent
characters.loreBio: A nameless Ishvalan monk whose people were systematically exterminated to satisfy a node in Father's transmutation array. His brother's dying act grafted two alchemical arms onto him — the right for Amestrian deconstruction, the left for Alkahestric reconstruction. He wages a decentralized war of retribution against State Alchemists before learning the true enemy lies beneath Central.
characters.systemBio: A hybrid-protocol insurgent node operating outside institutional frameworks. His blended arm arrays combine Amestrian destructive deconstruction with Alkahestric chi-routing, making him one of the few combatants who retains operational capability when Father executes his network-wide denial-of-service. Transitions from decentralized retribution to strategic infrastructure disruption.
characters.signatureMoment: Activating the reverse Nationwide Transmutation Circle using his brother's research — turning the weapon designed to destroy Amestris into the mechanism that saves it, completing the work of a dead man's faith.
characters.description: Scar is an Ishvalan warrior who survived a massacre and now hunts State Alchemists using a destructive alchemy tattooed on his right arm, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.

characters.name: Van Hohenheim
characters.title: The Counter-Network Architect
characters.loreBio: Father's exact inverse — bound to the other half of the Xerxesian souls, possessing identical raw power but operating through cooperative dialogue rather than authoritarian suppression. He spent four centuries conversing individually with every soul inside him, earning their consent. His counter-circle, planted throughout Amestris, is designed to neutralize Father's ritual at the moment of activation.
characters.systemBio: A decentralized counter-network with energy capacity equivalent to the parasitic root process. Achieves systemic resilience through cooperative soul management — each internal node operates as a willing participant rather than a suppressed battery. This consensus architecture enables precision failsafe deployment and maintains structural coherence under conditions that cause the authoritarian model to collapse from internal rebellion.
characters.signatureMoment: Confronting Father on the Promised Day — activating the counter-circle at the exact moment of the Nationwide Transmutation, releasing the stolen Amestrian souls from Father's container through a network of Stone fragments planted over centuries.
characters.description: Van Hohenheim is the biological father of Edward and Alphonse, an immortal alchemist who has lived for over 400 years seeking to free the souls trapped in Xerxes, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.
relationships: Fullmetal Alchemist: The Conqueror of Shamballa

characters.name: Ling Yao
characters.title: The Foreign Injection Vector
characters.loreBio: A Xingese prince who crosses the Great Desert seeking the Philosopher's Stone to secure his clan's survival in a 43-heir succession war. After being forcibly merged with the Homunculus Greed, he becomes a unique hybrid — a human consciousness directing a homunculus body, wielding the Ultimate Shield of carbon hardening and Stone-fueled regeneration while retaining his own tactical ambition.
characters.systemBio: A foreign protocol injection that introduces uncorrupted operational logic into the compromised Amestrian grid. Post-merger with Greed, he becomes a hybrid node possessing homunculus-grade defensive capabilities directed by human strategic cognition — the only entity that successfully absorbs and redirects a sub-routine of Father's network against the parent process.
characters.signatureMoment: Greed's final act — using his Ultimate Shield to carbonize Father's body from the inside, sacrificing his own Stone to create the structural weakness that allows Edward to deliver the final blow.
characters.description: Ling Yao is the crown prince of Xing who seeks immortality through Father's philosopher's stone, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.

characters.name: May Chang
characters.title: The Firewall Bypass
characters.loreBio: The youngest princess of the weakest Xingese clan, who crosses the desert with her tiny panda Xiao-Mei seeking immortality to save her family from political extinction. Her Alkahestry — channeling the Dragon's Pulse through kunai markers — operates on an entirely separate energy grid from Amestrian alchemy, making her critical when Father shuts down the tectonic network.
characters.systemBio: The primary vector for Alkahestric protocol injection into the Amestrian theater. Her chi-based transmutation operates on the planet's organic energy grid, completely independent of Father's parasitic layer — making her one of the only operational combatants during the network-wide denial-of-service event. Provides long-range healing and routing capabilities unavailable in the Amestrian protocol stack.
characters.signatureMoment: Performing healing transmutation during the Promised Day battle while all Amestrian alchemy is disabled — proving that her chi-based system exists on a completely independent energy grid.
characters.description: May Chang is a talented alkahest user from Xing who accompanies Ling on his quest, from Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood.

relationships.loreDesc: The structural foundation of the entire narrative — two brothers bound by shared guilt and mutual sacrifice, each willing to trade everything to restore the other. Their bond is the system's proof that equivalent exchange extends beyond chemistry into human connection.
relationships.systemDesc: A co-dependent anomaly pair whose mutual restoration objective drives the primary narrative thread. Each brother functions as a failsafe for the other — Edward's arm for Alphonse's soul, Alphonse's soul for Edward's arm — creating a closed-loop exchange system.

relationships.loreDesc: Edward's investigation of the Philosopher's Stone leads him to discover that Amestris itself is a constructed slaughterhouse. His rebellion against Father is both personal and systemic — dismantling the conspiracy that killed his predecessors and weaponized his country.
relationships.systemDesc: The primary adversarial edge between the subversive node and the parasitic root process. Edward's Gate-survivor status makes him both a target (required Human Sacrifice) and a threat (capable of understanding and countering the system's architecture).

relationships.loreDesc: Born from the same event, bound to the same souls, possessing identical raw power — but operating on opposite philosophies. Hohenheim dialogues with his souls; Father dominates his. This divergence defines the outcome of the Promised Day.
relationships.systemDesc: Perfect architectural mirrors with inverse resource management models. Authoritarian centralization (Father) versus cooperative decentralization (Hohenheim). The system ultimately validates the distributed model — Father's suppressed souls rebel and tear him apart, while Hohenheim's consenting network executes with precision.

relationships.loreDesc: Father created Bradley as the political face of the conspiracy — a mortal homunculus who commands the military, engineers wars, and maintains the institutional facade that conceals the transmutation circle being carved into the nation.
relationships.systemDesc: A top-down control dependency where the root process deploys a hybrid administrative node to manage the institutional apparatus. Bradley's human biology makes him non-regenerative but psychologically authentic — the ideal interface between shadow control and public governance.

relationships.loreDesc: Mustang's ambition to become Führer and reform the military puts him on a direct collision course with Bradley — the man who embodies the system Mustang swore to dismantle. Their rivalry is institutional as much as personal.
relationships.systemDesc: An institutional succession conflict between the reform-oriented node and the incumbent administrative interface. Mustang's parallel loyalty network directly threatens Bradley's control architecture, creating an inevitable adversarial convergence.

relationships.loreDesc: Scar hunts State Alchemists as retribution for the Ishvalan genocide — and Mustang was one of the soldiers who executed that genocide. Their relationship evolves from lethal enmity to reluctant alliance as both recognize the true enemy beneath Central.
relationships.systemDesc: An adversarial edge that transitions to tactical cooperation when both nodes identify the shared root-level threat. Their initial conflict (retribution vs institutional loyalty) resolves into functional alignment against the parasitic process that weaponized them both.

relationships.loreDesc: Scar initially targets Edward as a State Alchemist — a cog in the machine that destroyed Ishval. Their violent encounters gradually shift as Edward proves willing to confront the system rather than serve it, transforming Scar from hunter into reluctant co-belligerent.
relationships.systemDesc: A recalibrating adversarial relationship where the insurgent node's targeting criteria evolve from institutional affiliation to actual complicity. Edward's demonstrated anti-systemic behavior eventually reclassifies him from valid target to compatible resistance node.

relationships.loreDesc: Mustang recruited Edward into the State Alchemist program, giving the brothers access to military resources for their quest. Their alliance is pragmatic — Edward tolerates the military's leash because Mustang's reform ambitions align with his own need to dismantle the conspiracy.
relationships.systemDesc: A mutually instrumental alliance where the institutional reformer provides resource access to the subversive investigator. Each node leverages the other's position — Mustang gains intelligence from Edward's field operations, Edward gains institutional cover from Mustang's rank.

relationships.loreDesc: Edward despises his absent father for abandoning the family — not understanding that Hohenheim left to deploy counter-measures against a threat that would consume the entire nation. Their reconciliation is the emotional core of the Promised Day.
relationships.systemDesc: A delayed knowledge-transfer edge where the counter-network architect's strategic context is withheld from the subversive node until late-stage convergence. Hohenheim's absence was operationally necessary but created an adversarial trust deficit that only resolves under existential threat conditions.

relationships.loreDesc: Ling came to Amestris seeking the Philosopher's Stone for his clan's survival. After being forcibly merged with Greed, he redirects the homunculus's power against the very entity that created it — turning Father's own sub-routine into a weapon aimed back at him.
relationships.systemDesc: A foreign injection vector that captures and repurposes a sub-routine from the parasitic network. The Ling-Greed hybrid demonstrates that Father's compartmentalized architecture contains exploitable nodes that can be redirected against the parent process.

relationships.loreDesc: May's alkahestric knowledge and Alphonse's alchemical understanding create a cross-protocol exchange that enriches both. Their alliance introduces the Dragon's Pulse methodology into the Amestrian resistance, providing the firewall bypass critical to the Promised Day.
relationships.systemDesc: A cross-protocol knowledge exchange between the chi-based Alkahestric vector and the tectonic-based Amestrian anomaly. This edge enables protocol translation and introduces firewall-immune capabilities into the resistance network.

relationships.loreDesc: Bradley hunts Scar as a threat to institutional stability — but Scar's deconstruction alchemy and guerrilla tactics make him one of the few combatants who can challenge Bradley's speed. Their final battle strips both to their most fundamental drives.
relationships.systemDesc: A direct confrontation between the administrative interface and the insurgent node. Bradley's sensory superiority is countered by Scar's hybrid protocol stack, creating one of the few matchups where the homunculus's lack of regeneration becomes a decisive vulnerability.

factions.name: Amestris State Military
factions.loreDesc: A hyper-militarized imperialist state modeled on early 20th-century Prussia. The military licenses State Alchemists as human weapons, engineers border conflicts, and maintains an authoritarian hierarchy — all unknowingly serving as construction infrastructure for Father's 400-year transmutation circle. The wars are not for territory; they are for blood.
factions.systemDesc: An institutional apparatus designed as unwitting construction and harvesting infrastructure for the parasitic root process. Every war, every border conflict, every State Alchemist deployment serves the covert goal of generating Crests of Blood at precise geographic coordinates. The banality of evil as operating system.

factions.name: Father's Network
factions.loreDesc: Seven Homunculi — each an embodiment of Father's purged sins — enforce a 400-year conspiracy from the shadows. Pride surveils, Envy destabilizes, Sloth excavates, Wrath administrates, Lust infiltrates, Gluttony consumes, and Greed defects. Together they maintain the parasitic infrastructure beneath Amestris.
factions.systemDesc: A compartmentalized sub-routine network where each node performs a specialized function in service of the root process's extraction algorithm. The network's rigid top-down architecture enables total coordination but introduces catastrophic fragility — each sub-routine operates on hardcoded directives with no capacity for autonomous adaptation when the parent process fails.

factions.name: Xing Empire
factions.loreDesc: An eastern monarchy locked in a savage 43-heir succession crisis. The Emperor's failing health drives princes and princesses across the Great Desert to Amestris, seeking the Philosopher's Stone as a path to immortality and political survival. They inject foreign alchemical methodology — Alkahestry — into the Amestrian grid.
factions.systemDesc: A foreign political architecture that introduces uncorrupted protocol logic into the compromised Amestrian system. The succession crisis functions as a biological filter, selecting for extreme motivation and tactical versatility. Their Alkahestric methodology operates on an independent energy grid, making Xingese actors structurally immune to Father's control mechanisms.

factions.name: Briggs Fortress
factions.loreDesc: A military garrison on the frozen northern border that operates as a near-independent micro-state under Major General Olivier Armstrong. Briggs enforces a Darwinian meritocracy where rank follows competence alone. Their deep institutional suspicion of Central Command makes them the only military unit structurally resistant to Father's political manipulation.
factions.systemDesc: An isolated, self-reliant military node whose environmental hardening and ideological independence create natural resistance to the parasitic control network. Their localized survival optimization and institutional distrust of the central hierarchy make them the most viable institutional ally for the counter-systemic resistance.

factions.name: Ishvalan Survivors
factions.loreDesc: The scattered remnants of a civilization systematically exterminated to satisfy a node in Father's transmutation array. Their faith in Ishvala forbade alchemy — the very science used to destroy them. Scar wages decentralized retribution, while other survivors preserve their culture in displacement, awaiting the chance to reclaim their homeland.
factions.systemDesc: A displaced population node whose geographic position on a required Blood Crest made them a target for systemic deletion. Their anti-alchemical theology and decentralized resistance model make them structurally unpredictable within Father's institutional control framework — insurgent nodes that operate outside the conventional command hierarchy.

rules.name: Law of Equivalent Exchange
rules.subtitle: The Governing Thermodynamic Absolute
rules.loreConsequence: To obtain, something of equal value must be lost. This is not a guideline — it is the universe's operating system. Every transmutation, every sacrifice, every gain is weighed on a cosmic scale. The law cannot be broken, only deferred — and deferred costs always come due with interest.
rules.systemEquivalent: The hard thermodynamic constraint governing all energy transactions in the system. No operation can generate net-positive output — every transformation requires equivalent input. The Philosopher's Stone appears to violate this law but merely internalizes the cost as soul-unit consumption, maintaining the ledger's balance through hidden accounting.

rules.name: The Gate's Toll
rules.subtitle: Ironic Sacrifice for Forbidden Knowledge
rules.loreConsequence: Any alchemist who commits Human Transmutation is dragged before Truth and forced to pay a physical toll that perfectly mirrors their deepest desire. Edward lost his leg (his foundation) and arm (his reach). Alphonse lost his entire body (his ability to feel). Izumi lost her reproductive organs (her ability to nurture). The toll is not random — it is precisely ironic.
rules.systemEquivalent: A root-level audit response that detects taboo operations, forcibly transfers universal knowledge, and extracts a calibrated physical resource as payment. The toll's ironic precision demonstrates that Truth operates with full read-access to the violator's psychological profile, selecting the deduction that inflicts maximum systemic disruption to their core objective function.
rules.systemSubtitle: Taboo-Triggered Audit with Precision Toll Extraction

rules.name: Taboo of Human Transmutation
rules.subtitle: The Absolute Prohibition
rules.loreConsequence: Attempting to resurrect the dead is the one act that triggers direct intervention from Truth. The transmutation always fails — a human soul cannot be synthesized or retrieved from the afterlife. What returns is never the intended person. But the act itself opens the alchemist's Gate, granting them universal knowledge and the ability to transmute without circles.
rules.systemEquivalent: An operation that requests a resource type (human soul) that exists outside the system's addressable memory. The request triggers a catastrophic rebound, an enforced audit, and a toll — but as a side-effect, the failed operation writes the universal instruction set into the user's local memory, permanently upgrading their execution capability.
rules.loreSubtitle: The Sin That Opens the Gate

rules.name: Father's Alchemical Firewall
rules.subtitle: Hidden Energy Layer Substitution
rules.loreConsequence: Every Amestrian alchemist believes they draw power from the Earth's tectonic energy. In reality, Father has inserted his Philosopher's Stone network between alchemists and the true energy source — making every transmutation a dependency call to his infrastructure. He can revoke access at will, executing a nationwide denial-of-service that renders all Amestrian alchemy inert.
rules.systemEquivalent: A man-in-the-middle interception layer that substitutes the native energy source with a controlled proxy. Users cannot distinguish the artificial source from the natural one under normal conditions. The parasitic layer provides a kill-switch capability — the root process can terminate all dependent operations instantaneously by halting energy flow from the proxy Stone.

rules.name: Homunculus Attrition Ceiling
rules.subtitle: Finite Regeneration from Finite Souls
rules.loreConsequence: Homunculi regenerate from fatal wounds by burning trapped souls from their Philosopher's Stone. This regeneration is not infinite — every death consumes soul-units. Inflict enough lethal damage fast enough, and the Stone depletes. When it reaches zero, the Homunculus dies permanently. Their immortality is a budget, not a guarantee.
rules.systemEquivalent: A regenerative process funded by a finite resource pool. Each recovery cycle decrements the internal soul-counter. The system's apparent invulnerability is an illusion of scale — a sufficiently high-throughput attacker can force depletion through sustained, unbroken damage output that exceeds the entity's retreat and adaptation capacity.
rules.loreSubtitle: Kill Them Enough Times and They Stay Dead

counterplay.loreDesc: When Father shuts off Amestrian alchemy by halting his Stone's energy flow, Alkahestry practitioners retain full capability. May Chang and Scar's hybrid arms channel the Dragon's Pulse — an organic chi network completely independent of Father's artificial layer — continuing to fight while elite State Alchemists stand powerless.

Hohenheim spent centuries planting Philosopher's Stone fragments at precise intersections throughout Amestris, creating a shadow counter-circle that activates at the exact moment Father's ritual fires. The counter-network hijacks the activation sequence, reversing the soul extraction and returning fifty million stolen souls to their bodies.

Roy Mustang's precision flame alchemy subjects Homunculi to continuous, unbroken cycles of incineration — boiling eyes, charring flesh, and forcing the Stone to burn soul after soul in rapid succession. Against Lust, this attrition strategy depletes her entire Stone in minutes. Against Envy, it reduces a towering monster to a pathetic, palm-sized worm.

Because Wrath lacks regeneration, counterplay against him cannot rely on attrition. Instead, combatants block his sightlines with environmental hazards, coordinate overwhelming multi-directional strikes to overload his reaction time, and exploit his aging body's stamina limits. The strategy shifts from outlasting a monster to cornering a perfect but mortal tactician.

Scar's right arm executes Amestrian-style deconstruction while his left arm channels Alkahestric reconstruction — a hybrid stack that combines the offensive lethality of one system with the firewall immunity of the other. Against conventional alchemists, he simply touches and disintegrates; against Father's shutoff, he remains fully operational.

Edward discovers the one toll Truth cannot refuse — his own Gate of Truth. By sacrificing his ability to perform alchemy forever, he offers something of genuine equivalent value for Alphonse's body. It is the only trade Truth approves with a smile, because Edward finally understands that the Gate itself was never the point.

anomalies.name: The Gate of Truth
anomalies.loreDesc: A metaphysical boundary containing the totality of alchemical knowledge and the Akashic records. Those who commit Human Transmutation are dragged into its white void and confronted by Truth — a featureless entity that forces universal knowledge into the violator's consciousness before extracting a precisely ironic physical toll.
anomalies.systemDesc: The system's root-level audit interface — a construct that stores the universal instruction set and enforces thermodynamic compliance through mandatory toll extraction. The Gate itself is unique to each user, its appearance reflecting their individual understanding of the universe, suggesting that the 'boundary' is a subjective construct mapped onto an objective enforcement mechanism.

anomalies.name: Philosopher's Stone
anomalies.loreDesc: Appears to bypass Equivalent Exchange by generating massive transmutations without visible input. In reality, the cost is internalized — each use violently consumes trapped human souls. The Stone is not a miracle but a hidden ledger entry: the exchange still balances, the price is just paid by someone who cannot refuse.
anomalies.systemDesc: An energy reservoir that disguises its transaction costs by drawing from a pre-loaded, non-renewable resource pool. The 'violation' of Equivalent Exchange is an accounting illusion — the thermodynamic ledger remains balanced, but the cost is shifted from the operator to the stored soul-units, creating a moral debt invisible to the system's physics.

anomalies.name: Human Sacrifices
anomalies.loreDesc: The five alchemists who survived their encounter with the Gate of Truth — Edward, Alphonse, Izumi, Hohenheim, and Mustang — retain open connections to the universal code. Father requires their resonating Gates as tuning forks to crack open the Gate of the Earth itself during the Promised Day, making them both targets and essential components of the apocalypse.
anomalies.systemDesc: Anomalous nodes that maintain persistent connections to the root-level audit system after surviving unauthorized access attempts. Their open Gate connections generate the resonant frequency needed to bridge between the individual audit interface and the planetary-scale Gate — making them the critical bottleneck in Father's extraction algorithm and his greatest single point of failure.

anomalies.name: Circleless Transmutation
anomalies.loreDesc: Alchemists who survive the Gate retain the ability to transmute without drawing circles — they clap their hands to complete the circuit through their own bodies. This makes them tactically superior, removing the preparation time and vulnerability of circle-drawing. Only Gate survivors and Philosopher's Stone users can bypass this fundamental constraint.
anomalies.systemDesc: A permanent capability upgrade granted as a side-effect of surviving root-level audit access. The user's body becomes the geometric matrix, eliminating the need for external interface hardware. This creates a clear tactical tier division between standard practitioners (circle-dependent) and taboo survivors (somatic circuit-completion).

causalEvents.name: The Fall of Xerxes
causalEvents.trigger: The Dwarf in the Flask manipulates the Xerxesian king's desire for immortality, tricking him into carving a nationwide transmutation array disguised as an irrigation network.
causalEvents.consequence: Over one million souls are harvested. The nation is instantly destroyed. Father and Hohenheim emerge as twin entities bound to opposing halves of the soul reservoir — establishing the parasitic architecture that will define the next four centuries.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The beta-test for planetary-scale soul extraction. An entire civilization erased in a single transmutation, proving that the Philosopher's Stone mechanism scales to national populations and that institutional trust can be weaponized for mass sacrifice.
causalEvents.systemDesc: The initial proof-of-concept deployment that validates the mass soul-extraction algorithm and establishes the twin-entity architecture: one authoritarian node (Father) and one cooperative node (Hohenheim), each carrying half the harvested energy reserve.

causalEvents.name: The Ishvalan War of Extermination
causalEvents.trigger: The Homunculus Envy, disguised as an Amestrian soldier, murders an Ishvalan child — deliberately engineering a seven-year war that culminates in full-scale genocide when State Alchemists are deployed as weapons of mass destruction.
causalEvents.consequence: A Blood Crest is carved at the eastern node of the transmutation circle. The genocide traumatizes State Alchemists like Mustang and the Armstrongs, radicalizes survivors like Scar, and accelerates the militarization of alchemy — all serving Father's algorithm.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The architectural genocide — an entire people exterminated not for territory or ideology but to paint a single point on a geometric array. The soldiers who pulled the triggers never knew they were drawing a transmutation circle with human blood.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A deliberately engineered population-scale trauma event that satisfies a topological requirement (Blood Crest at eastern node) while simultaneously advancing three secondary objectives: combat-testing alchemical weapons, generating psychological pressure on potential Human Sacrifices, and eliminating a population whose anti-alchemical theology threatened institutional compliance.

causalEvents.name: The Elric Transmutation
causalEvents.trigger: Eleven-year-old Edward and ten-year-old Alphonse attempt to resurrect their dead mother using Human Transmutation — violating the ultimate taboo.
causalEvents.consequence: Both brothers pass through the Gate of Truth. Edward loses his leg and sacrifices his arm to bind Alphonse's soul to armor. Both gain circleless transmutation. Their quest for the Philosopher's Stone draws them into the conspiracy, and their Gate-survivor status makes them required Human Sacrifices for Father's ritual.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Two children trying to bring back their mother become the catalyst for everything that follows. Their grief generates exactly the kind of anomaly Father's algorithm requires — alchemists desperate enough to open the Gate and survive.
causalEvents.systemDesc: An unauthorized taboo operation that generates two Gate-survivor anomalies with persistent root-level connections. The event simultaneously creates Father's required Human Sacrifice nodes and the subversive agents who will ultimately dismantle his infrastructure — the algorithm's greatest asset and greatest vulnerability produced by the same transaction.

causalEvents.name: The Promised Day
causalEvents.trigger: The solar eclipse creates a cosmic gateway. Father activates the completed Nationwide Transmutation Circle, forces the five Human Sacrifices to resonate their Gates, and absorbs fifty million Amestrian souls along with the entity of God.
causalEvents.consequence: Father achieves momentary omnipotence. Hohenheim's counter-circle activates, releasing the stolen souls. The resistance exploits Father's destabilized container. Edward sacrifices his Gate to retrieve Alphonse. Father is dragged back through the Gate by Truth, returned to the void he originally escaped.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Four hundred years of engineered atrocity converge in a single day. The parasite achieves its goal, the counter-network fires, and the system's final verdict is delivered by Truth itself — proving that no amount of stolen power can override the ledger.
causalEvents.systemDesc: The terminal convergence event where the parasitic extraction algorithm executes against the planetary energy grid, the decentralized counter-network activates its failsafe sequence, and the root-level arbiter (Truth) enforces final thermodynamic compliance — stripping the parasitic process of all stolen resources and returning it to its original null state.

systemQuestions.question: How does alchemy work at a system level?
systemQuestions.answer: Alchemy is a transformation protocol bounded by equivalent exchange, so gains are always paid through material or structural cost.

systemQuestions.question: Why is equivalent exchange more than a slogan?
systemQuestions.answer: The rules section shows it as a hard accounting layer that limits shortcuts and defines failure states.

systemQuestions.question: How did Amestris become a national transmutation engine?
systemQuestions.answer: Causal events connect military expansion, sacrifice logistics, and state doctrine into one engineered pipeline.

systemQuestions.question: Which factions drive or resist the core mechanism?
systemQuestions.answer: Faction cards map state, resistance, and homunculus incentives that keep the system stable or push it toward collapse.

systemQuestions.question: Best way to read FMAB in this archive?
systemQuestions.answer: Start with Power Engine, then jump to Causal Events to see how transmutation rules scale from lab practice to geopolitical design.

hero.microHook: Track control edges before reading motives.
hero.thesis: FMAB is structurally defined by parasitic control networks — Father routes energy through a hidden layer beneath Amestris, homunculi enforc…

introductionSummary: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood runs on a thermodynamic ledger — every creation demands equivalent destruction — that a 400-year parasite has been systematically rigging from beneath the earth. Father's hidden control layer beneath Amestris, the homunculi's institutional dependency network, and the State Alchemists' unknowingly hijacked grid all map as nodes in a control web designed to keep the ledger permanently off-balance. The node-graph best shows who bypasses Father's firewall and how the counter-network assembles to collapse it.
